trivia: The flying wing that redefined what aircraft can be: no tail, no fuselage, nearly invisible to radar. Around 19 exist, each named after a US state, each worth more than most airlines.
A stealth bomber in a hobbyist's log book is the ultimate irony catch: transponders on, mystery off.
funFacts: Each aircraft is named Spirit of a US state.
Its wingspan matches the 1940s YB-49 flying wing almost exactly.
